name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-18
title: Consolidate standard workflow guidance into rabbit-workspace-map (onboarding surface for non-developer users)
description:
## Source

Filed during the 2026-05-18 brainstorm for CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1 (prompt management interface). User decided that workflow-guidance UX is a separate concern from prompt management and asked to backlog it here.

## Goal

Give users — especially non-developers using rabbit via the .rabbit/-as-plugin model (CONTRACT-BACKLOG-17) — an intuitive way to learn the standard rabbit workflow:

- How to use rabbit-feature-touch and what each TDD step does
- How to bypass human-approval (and the implications)
- Where to file bugs / backlog items (rabbit-file)
- How to switch between standalone and plugin modes (after CONTRACT-BACKLOG-17)
- Etc.

This is distinct from the prompt-management UX (CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1) — that one is for power users / developers tuning subagent prompts. Workflow guidance is for everyone.

## Why consolidate into rabbit-workspace-map

rabbit-workspace-map already exposes the workspace hierarchy in human-readable form. It is the natural home for "what is this workspace, what skills/features live here, how do I use them" — the next layer up is "what's the recommended workflow for common tasks."

Possible extensions:
- A --workflow / --how-to mode that renders the standard workflow as a guided sequence
- Per-feature workflow snippets surfaced when a feature is queried
- Hook to surface "next likely step" given current state

## Out of scope

- Prompt management UX (tracked separately as CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1)
- Authoring tutorial content (could be a follow-up once the surface is decided)

## Status

Backlogged for future brainstorm/design. Not blocking any current work. Implementation depends on CONTRACT-BACKLOG-17 (plugin mode detection) for full value, but standalone-mode pieces can land earlier.

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
explicit deferral during 2026-05-18 brainstorm meant 'future brainstorm', not 'abandon'; housekeeping mis-classified
Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

Obsolete: rabbit-workspace-map skill retired (commit 0b0553f9). The entire item was structured around that skill as the consolidation target; goal of non-developer onboarding UX preserved for a future fresh filing if desired.
